messages.text: After the wedding, Bassanio and Gratiano left for Venice with money enough to pay the debt many times. The urgent danger remained, but Portia watched them go calmly.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: Nerissa, we will not wait here like painted figures while men decide the life of Antonio.

messages.name: Nerissa
messages.text: What can we do? Venice has law, judges, and a contract already sealed.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: Then we must bring better law, sharper wisdom, and perhaps mercy hidden in a disguise.
She sent a servant to Padua to consult her cousin Bellario, a learned doctor of law. Books and clothing would return by speed.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: We will dress as young men. I will be a doctor; you shall be my clerk.

messages.name: Nerissa
messages.text: And if our husbands see us?

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: They will see what they expect to see. That is the oldest disguise in the world.
Portia trusted not only clothing, but attention. She had listened to the bond, the risk, and the gaps inside strict words.
Jessica and Lorenzo remained at Belmont, surprised by Portia's sudden journey and by the authority she carried without raising her voice.

messages.name: Jessica
messages.text: She moves as if kindness can become a strategy.

messages.name: Lorenzo
messages.text: Bassanio is fortunate in more than fortune.
On the road, Nerissa teased her mistress about the ring. Men swear loudly when happy and forget quickly when pressured.

messages.name: Nerissa
messages.text: If we save their friend, let us test whether they stay loyal to those rings.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: A small trial after a great one may teach them humility.
The plan was playful, but not empty. The ring marked trust, and trust needed memory under pressure.
Their carriage hurried toward Venice. In Portia's hands, love did not remain private; it became law, disguise, and action.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: If Shylock asks only justice, we will give him justice so exact that mercy may enter through the edge.
Nerissa looked at her mistress and smiled. The clerk had found her doctor, and the journey gathered speed.
Behind them Belmont stayed peaceful. Ahead of them the court prepared for a bond that seemed impossible to break.
Portia did not boast of her plan. Her confidence came from reading closely, a habit many men in the play mistake for their own privilege.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: The law is a house with many doors. Shylock has entered one; we must find another before it closes.

messages.name: Nerissa
messages.text: And if the court refuses two young strangers?

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: Then our disguise must carry age in its voice and wisdom in its papers. Bellario will lend both.
The ring test began as a jest, but Portia did not treat promises lightly. A loyal heart should survive gratitude as well as danger.

messages.name: Nerissa
messages.text: Men call women changeable, yet they misplace vows in a single afternoon.
Their journey turned marriage into partnership. While Bassanio raced toward mercy, Portia raced toward the means to make mercy lawful.
Bellario's books reached her before doubt could. Portia read through the night, turning scholarship into a weapon precise enough for court.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: We must not break the law. We must let the law reveal the part Shylock forgot to ask for.
Nerissa practiced the clerk's walk and voice. Their disguise depended on confidence, but also on a world too ready to trust male clothing.

messages.name: Nerissa
messages.text: If they call me learned, I shall try not to laugh.
The disguise also revealed a rule of her world: Portia could own wealth, but authority in court required a borrowed male shape.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: If Venice will not hear Portia, it may hear Balthazar. The truth can travel under another name.
Nerissa understood. Their comedy had a serious door hidden inside it.
